items.title: Police charge Tennant Creek teenager with 21-year-old's stabbing murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police charge a 17-year-old boy with the murder of a 21-year-old man who he allegedly stabbed to death in Tennant Creek last week.

image.altText: NT Police insignia on mud-splattered car door.

items.title: Alleged drug dealer caught after trading cannabis for cat food

children.content: Police charge a 45-year-old woman with a range of drug, weapons and other offences after a group of teenagers were caught stealing cat food to allegedly swap with her for cannabis.

image.altText: A large collection of bags full of cannabis, laid out on a carpet.

items.title: The one of a kind remote NT camp where 'it's cool to go to school'

children.content: Children from across Central Australia have joined a remote Northern Territory camp inside the Watarrka National Park where "it's cool to go to school" — and the footy doesn't hurt either.

gallery.altText: A child swings a baseball bat on a grassy, dirt field.
An aerial shot of a very small remote community with only a handful of buildings.

thumbnail.altText: A child swings a baseball bat on a grassy, dirt field.
An aerial shot of a very small remote community with only a handful of buildings.

items.title: Creditors of NT mine with $360m debt criticise lack of oversight

children.content: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

image.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

items.title: NT government says Claravale land-clearing dispute is now settled

children.content: For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit. But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

image.altText: Cleared land with two lone trees in the middle.

items.title: NT government axes controversial $1.1 million private midwife contract

children.content: The Northern Territory government has axed a $1.1 million contract awarded to a Darwin midwife, banned from working unsupervised, almost 10 months after doctors began raising concerns about the deal.

gallery.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: Police dog puts alleged fugitive bikie in hospital

children.content: A Top End bikie who allegedly escaped police custody last week has been hospitalised after being bitten by a police dog during his re-capture.

image.altText: A dog stands on the back seat of a ute.

items.title: NT government questioned over youth prison lockdowns, police policy

children.content: As the NT's CLP government remains firm on its tough-on-crime agenda and rebuilding of the economy, the first week of budget estimates saw the government questioned around prison lockdowns, police policy and housing.

image.altText: Two curved tables facing each other, people sitting either side, most in formal office wear.

items.title: Why this Socceroos squad is like no other

children.content: The Socceroos squad is like no other that's ever been sent to represent us at a World Cup. See the extraordinary journeys that forged a team to represent modern Australia on the world stage.

items.title: Scotland claims first World Cup win in 36 years as Brazil, Morocco share spoils

children.content: Brazil and Morocco share the spoils in a blockbuster clash after Qatar salvaged a late draw against Switzerland in San Francisco, while Scotland hung on to beat Haiti.

items.title: Here's what you need to know about the KPMG scandal

items.title: Nine-year-old Perth girl shot dead by police in Pakistan

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

items.title: The multi-million-dollar tourist hotspot where locals can't afford to live

items.title: Why young people say the social media ban is not working six months on

items.title: 'Straight to landfill': Compostable packaging but nowhere to compost it

items.title: Street signs link coastal town and luxury ocean liners dating back to 1800s

children.content: Orient Point, where the streets are named after ships beginning with "O", shows how national pride in 19th and early 20th-century boatbuilding featured in colonial town planning.
